title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "New Source Performance Standards Review for Volatile Organic Liquid Storage Vessels (Including Petroleum Liquid Storage Vessels)".
plain_summary: This bill would cancel a new EPA rule about air pollution from large tanks that store chemicals and oil. The EPA rule set stricter standards for new or changed storage tanks to reduce harmful air pollution. Congress can use this bill to reject the EPA rule and stop it from taking effect. If the bill passes, the EPA would not be able to enforce these new pollution standards for storage tanks. The rule was published in October 2024 and applies to tanks built or changed after October 2023.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress has decided to reject a rule made by the Environmental Protection Agency. This rule was called New Source Performance Standards Review for Volatile Organic Liquid Storage Vessels (Including Petroleum Liquid Storage Vessels). The rule was published in the Federal Register on October fifteenth, two thousand twenty-four. Because Congress is rejecting this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced. This means the rule will not become law or affect anyone.
